name: Sarah Chen

ap_courses.note: Taken at San Jose State, dual enrollment
Taken at San Jose State, dual enrollment

activities.name: Math Olympiad / AMC-AIME
activities.description: Qualified for AIME three consecutive years. AMC 12 top 1% nationally. Led weekly math circle for underclassmen.

activities.name: Machine Learning Research Internship
activities.description: Co-authored research paper on using NLP to identify learning gaps in K-8 math education. Paper accepted at a regional undergraduate research symposium. Worked under Dr. Emily Park, SJSU CS Department.
awards: Co-author: 'Automated Gap Analysis in Elementary Math Instruction Using Transformer Models' (2024)

activities.name: Science Olympiad
activities.description: Competed in Experimental Design, Circuit Lab, and Codebusters events. Led team to regional championship senior year.

activities.name: Piano
activities.description: Completed Royal Conservatory of Music Grade 10. Performed at Carnegie Hall in national youth recital program junior year. Teaches beginner piano to 3 neighborhood children on weekends.
awards: Carnegie Hall National Young Artists Recital

activities.name: Math Tutoring Program (Volunteer)
activities.description: Founded a free after-school tutoring program for students at a nearby Title I middle school. Recruited 8 student tutors from her high school. Currently serves 22 middle school students.

activities.name: Robotics Club
activities.description: Wrote autonomous navigation code for FRC robot. Team placed in regional finals two years running.

intended_career: AI research with applications in education or healthcare

essays.main_essay_theme: Reflects on how tutoring a struggling 7th grader exposed the limits of her own understanding of math — leading her to pursue ML research as a way to build adaptive learning systems. Technically sophisticated but warm; avoids jargon.
essays.supplemental_notes: Why Major essays are precise and specific. Why School essays are well-researched. One supplement on her piano performance is notably lyrical and shows range beyond STEM.
essays.writing_style: Clear, confident, occasionally dry. Strongest when discussing research. Personal voice emerges most in the piano essay.

hooks: None — no legacy, athletic, or development hook
additional_info: Youngest co-author in department history at SJSU for her research paper. Has not applied to any schools yet — regular decision only.

high_school.name: Lynbrook High School
high_school.committee_context: Hyper-competitive Silicon Valley public school where less than 8% of students finish with a 4.0 UW. Twenty-seven AP courses offered; average student takes 5+. A 4.0 UW here is genuine — grades are not inflated. Equivalent rigor to top private schools.
high_school.gpa_adjustment_note: 4.0 UW from Lynbrook carries significantly more weight than a 4.0 from an average school. This is among the hardest 4.0s in the country to earn.
